rationale: Zero npm/node dependency keeps Rekall pip-installable. Vanilla JS + CSS is sufficient for the event scale. Avoids build tooling that would complicate distribution.
title: Single-file HTML explorer with no framework
tradeoffs: Harder to componentize. No hot reload during dev. Large single file. But ships inside the Python package with zero friction.

rationale: Full-history lane spread wastes space and doesn't answer 'what caused this?' BFS neighborhood from selected event shows only causally relevant events.
title: Trace as causal neighborhood, not full timeline
tradeoffs: Loses the full-timeline overview. Users can't see the entire execution history in Trace. But the Ledger already provides that — Trace should explain, not duplicate.

rationale: Agents skip COMPULSORY FIRST STEP instructions. Proved it myself — read the instruction, ignored it. Hooks inject context without agent compliance.
title: SessionStart hook over instruction-based adoption
tradeoffs: Hook is Claude Code specific. Other IDEs need different enforcement (MCP gate, CLI stderr). Three-layer approach required instead of universal solution.

rationale: Deterministic state computation must be testable without I/O. Putting reduce() in its own module with only data arguments makes it a pure function with 36 unit tests.
title: Pure-function reducer separate from StateStore
tradeoffs: Extra module. StateStore.compute_state() is a thin wrapper. But reducer determinism is provable and the brief/policy/trace all benefit from the same computed state.

rationale: Shadow mode (allow+log) is not enough for enterprise. Real enforcement needs block and require_approval outcomes. But default must stay open (Tier 0: allow).
title: Policy evaluator with allow/warn/block/require_approval
tradeoffs: More complex policy engine. Risk of over-engineering governance before adoption. Mitigated by keeping it behind existing checkpoint/brief flows, not a new public surface.

rationale: Ledger narrows scope. Trace explains causality inside that scope. If Trace silently pulls events outside the time window, the filtered view becomes untrustworthy.
title: Time filtering scopes Trace, not just Ledger
tradeoffs: Users lose causal context from before the time window. Accepted: the Ledger already shows everything, and users can widen the window to get more context.

rationale: Agents only use checkpoint consistently because it has a natural trigger (task completion). Decisions and attempts have no trigger — they require the agent to notice and choose. Fix: make checkpoint responses remind agents, and make session-end audit for missing recordings.
title: Enforce decisions/attempts via session-end audit and checkpoint prompts
tradeoffs: Adds friction to session-end. Risk of agents learning to dismiss the audit. But the alternative — zero decisions recorded across 16 checkpoints — is worse.

rationale: guard/verify/session-end are never called because they have no natural trigger. Same compliance failure as decisions. Fix: bundle guard into the brief, verify into checkpoint, and session-end into a Claude Code hook.
title: Auto-guard at session start, auto-verify at checkpoint, auto-session-end via hook
tradeoffs: Adds latency to brief (guard check) and checkpoint (verify). Acceptable: both are fast and the value of catching issues early outweighs the cost.

rationale: 18 checkpoints, 0 external users. Enterprise features (policy, capabilities, approvals, rewind, explorer) are unvalidated. The wedge is cold-start elimination via brief + checkpoint. Everything else is premature.
title: Narrow product surface for distribution: hide enterprise features, focus on init-brief-checkpoint
tradeoffs: Loses the enterprise differentiation story short-term. But a simple tool that 100 people use beats a complex platform that 0 people use.
